channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Iceland votes for a new parliament after political disagreements force an early election
item.description: REYKJAVIK, Iceland (AP) — Icelanders voted to elect a new parliament Saturday after disagreements over immigration, energy policy and the economy forced Prime Minister Bjarni Benediktsson to pull the plug on his coalition government and call an early election.
item.dc:creator: MARCO DI MARCO and DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Islamist rebels in Syria catch Assad, Putin, Iran regimes off guard giving US new mideast headache
item.description: Amid a rapid-fire collapse of Syrian, Hezbollah, Russian and Iranian forces in northern Syria, questions are surfacing about U.S. policy toward the emerging new order in the war-ravaged Syrian Arab Republic.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli military says Hezbollah is smuggling weapons into Lebanon during cease-fire
item.description: Hezbollah terrorists reportedly are smuggling weapons across Lebanon's borders despite an ongoing cease-fire with Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Taiwan's president departs for Pacific visit with a 2-day stop in the US
item.description: TAOYUAN, Taiwan (AP) — Taiwan's president departed Saturday for a trip to the South Pacific that will include a two-day transit in the U.S., his first since assuming office.
item.dc:creator: TAIJING WU and HUIZHONG WU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel hits alleged Hezbollah weapons smuggling sites in Syria
item.description: Israeli military jets hit alleged Hezbollah weapons smuggling sites near the border crossing between Lebanon and Syria, according to officials, who allege the Lebanese militant group violated a ceasefire agreement.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: More than 100 arrested as Georgian police clash with protesters over suspension of EU talks
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — More than 100 demonstrators were arrested overnight in Georgia as protesters clashed with police following the government’s decision to suspend negotiations to join the European Union, the country’s Interior Ministry said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: In Bolivia's scrappy highlands, proud Indigenous Cholas take the runway by storm
item.description: In the huddled market stalls, sprawling farms and pulsing parties of Viacha, a scrappy town southeast of Bolivia’s capital, it’s typical for women to sport bowler hats, tiered skirts and fringed shawls.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Debre And Juan Karita, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: North Korea's Kim vows steadfast support for Russia’s war in Ukraine
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korean leader Kim Jong Un vowed his country will “invariably support” Russia’s war in Ukraine as he met Russia's defense chief, the North’s state media reported Saturday.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Imprisoned Kremlin critic convicted again, receives 3-year sentence for opposing war in Ukraine
item.description: Imprisoned Kremlin critic Alexei Gorinov was convicted again on Friday and sentenced to three years in prison for opposing Russia's ongoing war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lebanese fisherman hope ceasefire with Israel means normal life returning
item.description: The ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah is bringing hope for normality back to many in southern Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Bassem Mroue, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Romania's parliamentary vote risks being overshadowed by presidential race chaos
item.description: Romanians are preparing to go to the polls in a parliamentary vote that will determine a new government and prime minister to lead the European Union and NATO member country.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Mcgrath, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Stealth destroyer to be home for 1st hypersonic weapon on a US warship
item.description: The U.S. Navy is transforming a costly flub into a potent weapon with the first shipborne hypersonic weapon, which is being retrofitted aboard the first of its three stealthy destroyers.
item.dc:creator: DAVID SHARP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about sudden rebel gains in Syria's 13-year war and why it matters
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The 13-year civil war in Syria has roared back into prominence with a surprise rebel offensive on Aleppo, one of Syria's largest cities and an ancient business hub.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What Trump's Russia-Ukraine envoy has said about ending the war
item.description: President-elect Trump's choice for special envoy to the Russia-Ukraine war, Keith Kellogg, has offered fairly detailed proposals for ending the war that began almost three years ago.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Ireland votes in a close-run election where incumbents hope to cling on to power
item.description: Ireland is voting in a parliamentary election that will determine the country's next government; voters are selecting more than 170 lawmakers to sit in the Dail.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canadian news publishers sue OpenAI over alleged copyright infringement
item.description: OTTAWA, Ontario (AP) — A coalition of Canadian news publishers, including The Canadian Press, Torstar, Globe and Mail, Postmedia and CBC/Radio-Canada, has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI for using news content to train its ChatGPT generative artificial intelligence system.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: British lawmakers give initial approval to a bill to allow terminally ill adults to end their lives
item.description: LONDON (AP) — British lawmakers gave initial approval on Friday to a bill to help terminally ill adults end their lives in England and Wales, following an impassioned debate that saw people sharing personal stories of loss and suffering.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY and PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Stowaway who boarded New York-to-Paris flight will be sent back to US
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Authorities are investigating after a Russian national who did not have a ticket boarded a Paris-bound Delta flight at New York's John F. Kennedy International Airport this week and was arrested when the plane touched down.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: UK Cabinet member resigns over past phone fraud case
item.description: A member of the U.K. Cabinet has resigned after reports revealed she was convicted in a fraud case more than a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Amazon workers in India join Black Friday strike action for better wages and working conditions
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — Amazon staff in India have joined strike action calling for better wages and working conditions as the company prepares for one of the busiest shopping periods of the year.
item.dc:creator: SHONAL GANGULY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukrainian energy workers carry out repairs despite Russia's pounding of the country's power grid
item.description: On a bright winter day, workers at a Ukrainian thermal power plant repair its heavily damaged equipment as drops of water from melted snow leak through gaping holes in its battered roof.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin praises 'intelligent' Trump amid assassination threats
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin praised President-elect Trump as an “intelligent” and “experienced” leader Thursday, while noting he does not believe the president-elect is “safe” following the two attempts on his life earlier this year.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Notre Dame Cathedral unveils its new interior 5 years after devastating fire
item.description: PARIS (AP) — After more than five years of frenetic, but sometimes interrupted, reconstruction work, Notre Dame Cathedral showed itself anew to the world Friday, with rebuilt soaring ceilings and creamy good-as-new stonework erasing somber memories of its devastating fire in 2019.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON and JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran to begin enriching uranium with thousands of advanced centrifuges, UN watchdog says
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Iran will begin enriching uranium with thousands of advanced centrifuges at its two main nuclear facilities at Fordo and Natanz, the United Nations' nuclear watchdog said Friday, further raising tensions over Tehran's program as it enriches at near weapons-grade le
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN watchdog: Iran ramping up uranium enrichment efforts
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Iran will begin enriching uranium with thousands of advanced centrifuges at its two main nuclear facilities at Fordo and Natanz, the United Nations' nuclear watchdog said Friday, further raising tensions over Tehran's program as it enriches at near weapons-grade le
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, The Hill

item.title: Millions in Ukraine without power after Russia attacks grid
item.description: “Attacks on energy facilities are happening all over Ukraine,” Energy Minister Herman Halushchenko said on Facebook.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Powerful Mexican cartel accused of forced labor, human trafficking
item.description: The Gulf Cartel is a long-standing, powerful drug trafficking organization that operates throughout Tamaulipas.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation

item.title: Stowaway found on Delta flight from New York City to Paris
item.description: The TSA says the woman bypassed two identity verification areas and boarding status stations before getting on the plane.
item.dc:creator: Xavier Walton, NewsNation

item.title: Russia and Syria bomb Syrian Islamist rebels after surprise incursion
item.description: Syrian rebels launch attack against Hezbollah’s ally, Syria’s regime, amid Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hiker accidentally uncovers 280-million-year-old footprints in Italy
item.description: Hikers were responsible for the discovery of what scientists believe to be a prehistoric ecosystem. The first sign of the ecosystem discovered by the hikers were footprints.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Putin mulls striking Kyiv with new hypersonic missile that can reportedly reach US West Coast
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin says that Ukraine’s "decision-making centers" in Kyiv could be targeted next using a new hypersonic missile that could also potentially reach the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden admin urges Ukraine to lower draft age to conscript 18-year-olds
item.description: The Biden administration is urging Ukraine to lower its draft age to conscript 18-year-olds to keep pace with Russia’s growing military.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia launches another large missile, drone attack on Ukraine's energy infrastructure
item.description: Russia launched another attack on Ukraine's energy infrastructure on Thursday, leaving over a million households without power and forcing emergency power to be implemented nationwide.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump taps retired General Keith Kellogg for Ukraine envoy role
item.description: While there is currently no special envoy for the war in Ukraine, Trump had privately expressed interest in creating the position.
item.dc:creator: Gram Slattery, Svea Herbst-Bayliss and Steve Holland / Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: US sanctions 21 more Maduro allies accused of post-election repression in Venezuela
item.description: The U.S. has imposed sanctions on 21 more allies of Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro for repressing Venezuelans following the country's disputed presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Amelia Earhart’s plane photo not of the plane, company says
item.description: “After 11 months the waiting has finally ended and unfortunately our target was not Amelia's Electra 10E," the exploration company said.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli strikes in central Beirut kill at least 20 as diplomats push for a cease-fire
item.description: BEIRUT, Lebanon (AP) — Israeli airstrikes Saturday in central Beirut killed at least 20 people, officials said, as the once-rare attacks on the heart of Lebanon's capital continued without warning while diplomats scrambled to broker a cease-fire.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, WGN 9
